items.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` v1.6.0-beta.1, specifically requesting that the CHANGELOG, README Key concepts section, and Examples section be updated to reflect the new resource types introduced by the `2026-01-15-preview` API version upgrade.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` v1.6.0-beta.1  
**Affected API:** CHANGELOG.md, README.md, and sample files under `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/`  
**Scenarios:**
- CHANGELOG entry only mentions the API version bump, not the new resource types added
- README Key concepts section is generic, missing descriptions of Agent and RAI resource categories
- README Examples section has no code samples for any resource type, including newly added ones
- No sample files exist in `samples/` or `tests/Samples/` to support snippet injection

**Root ask:** Expand CHANGELOG, update README Key concepts, add code samples for the new resource types (`CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationResource`, `CognitiveServicesAgentDeploymentResource`, `RaiExternalSafetyProviderSchemaResource`, `RaiToolLabelResource`, `RaiTopicResource`, `SubscriptionRaiPolicyResource`).
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This issue was auto-generated by the Update Docs workflow triggered by PR #57510 (API version upgrade). The gaps are documentation-only — no behavioral bugs were reported.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md` and confirm the 1.6.0-beta.1 entry lacks enumeration of new resource types.
2. Review `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` Key concepts and Examples sections for completeness.
3. Implement changes per the Implementation Guide in the issue body: update CHANGELOG, Key concepts, add a sample file, and update Examples section.
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitiveservices` after adding snippet regions and verify README is updated.
5. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to validate no regressions.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — package name starts with `Azure.ResourceManager.`, which is the canonical indicator for management-plane SDKs
- **Service:** `Cognitive - Mgmt` — this is the established service label for `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` as seen in CODEOWNERS (line 590); confirmed label color #e99695 (service label)
- **Confidence:** High — the package name unambiguously maps to a single management SDK and a single service label; no ambiguity between categories or services
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Mgmt` (line 903) — catch-all for all Mgmt-category issues; scanning bottom-to-top (line 1228 → line 903), no more-specific combined entry `%Cognitive - Mgmt %Mgmt` exists in the file
- **AzureSdkOwners:** `@ArthurMa1978`
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed at line 903
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@ArthurMa1978` (single AzureSdkOwner); no "needs-team-attention" added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** Lines 954–1228 contain entries like `%Compute - Fleet %Mgmt`, `%ARM %Mgmt`, etc., all requiring a different service label alongside `%Mgmt` — none match "Cognitive - Mgmt". The more-specific entry `%Cognitive - Mgmt` at line 590 is before the `%Mgmt` catch-all; in bottom-to-top scanning, the catch-all at line 903 is encountered first and matches on "Mgmt" alone.
</details>
